_schema.purpose: Registry of council member constituent newsletters reviewed for the scorecard. Newsletters are official communications sent from city email addresses to constituent lists. They are a direct window into how members frame issues for voters — what they emphasize, what they omit, and whether their public framing matches their voting record. Several incidents in incidents.json are sourced from newsletters.

status_values.pending_review: Newsletter identified but not yet extracted
status_values.extracted: Key content captured in extract file or incident
status_values.scored: Content reflected in incidents.json or member scoring

fields.subject: Newsletter subject line or title
fields.scoring_signals: Specific statements or omissions that are scorecard-relevant
fields.incident_refs: List of incident entries in incidents.json sourced from this newsletter

taplin_march_2026.subject: March 2026 District 2 Newsletter
taplin_march_2026.key_content: Promoted community input meetings for a potential 2026 infrastructure bond; framed bond as the mechanism for addressing Berkeley's street and facility needs; included 0.5% sales tax community survey promotion
scoring_signals: Bond framed as primary solution to infrastructure needs — no mention of general fund reprioritization
Sales tax survey asks what residents want funded, not what they would cut
No reference to the October 2025 City Auditor streets audit finding that GF underfunding, not lack of bond authority, is the root cause

tregub_march_2026_part2.subject: March 2026 Part 2 Newsletter — District 4
tregub_march_2026_part2.key_content: Told constituents that passage of both a $300M infrastructure bond AND a half-cent sales tax was 'a sobering fact...essential to ensure the city's continued ability to deliver on the quality services that you deserve'
scoring_signals: Revenue-first framing with no mention of reprioritization alternatives
'Essential' characterization presented as fiscal necessity, not as a policy choice
No acknowledgment that the bond cycle has not improved street conditions over five prior measures

tregub_july_2025.subject: July 2025 District 4 Newsletter
tregub_july_2025.key_content: Called on constituents to attend an Alameda County Board of Supervisors meeting in support of Measure W homelessness funding
scoring_signals: Official constituent email list used to lobby for a county-level spending measure
Measure W would preferentially direct county funds to cities implementing Housing First — i.e., Berkeley
City and county measures are additive obligations on the same taxpayer base
